scenario: Kernel detects soft lockup at net_rx_action
symptoms: watchdog: BUG: soft lockup - CPU#6 stuck for 23s! [NetworkManager:1081]
Kernel panic - not syncing: Hard LOCKUP

workflow.thought: Check loaded kernel modules and their taint status
workflow.observation: Identify tainted modules like igb with O flag

workflow.thought: Search kernel log for lockup and RCU stall messages
workflow.observation: Find repeated soft lockups and eventual hard lockup panic

workflow.thought: Compare hrtimer interrupt counts to identify CPU
workflow.observation: Capture current hrtimer interrupt counts per CPU

workflow.thought: Compare with saved hrtimer interrupt counts
workflow.observation: Capture saved hrtimer interrupt counts per CPU

workflow.thought: Find common CPUs between current and saved counts
workflow.observation: Identify CPUs with matching interrupt counts

workflow.thought: Examine backtrace of CPU 4 to understand panic context
workflow.observation: Check panic call chain and exception RIP at vprintk_emit

workflow.thought: Disassemble vprintk_emit to understand spinlock issue
workflow.observation: Examine console_owner and console_waiter access patterns
Get task address of console owner
Set current process to PID 1081 for examination

workflow.thought: Disassemble net_rx_action to understand poll_owner lock
Calculate poll_owner address from napi pointer
Examine napi pointer from stack frame

workflow.observation: Confirm napi pointer matches earlier value

root_cause_analysis: netpoll_poll_dev calls igb_poll() with napi->poll_owner locking.
However, net_rx_action() called by igb_poll tries to lock the napi->poll_owner itself.
This deadlock issue was probably caused because the 3rd party igb module did unexpected behavior or invocation.
